content.content:
1→# Naming Map and Glossary
     2→
     3→> **Abstract:** This document provides the authoritative naming map for all Roko
     4→> terminology, mapping legacy names to their current equivalents and defining every
     5→> Roko-specific term, abbreviation, and architectural concept used across the PRD suite.
     6→> When any other document disagrees with a definition here, this glossary is authoritative.
     7→> This serves as a quick-reference companion to the full architecture documentation.
     8→
     9→
    10→> **Implementation**: Shipping
    11→
    12→---
    13→
    14→## 1. Project and Framework Names
    15→
    16→The Roko project has undergone several naming iterations. The following table is the
    17→canonical old-to-new mapping. All documentation uses the new names exclusively (with
    18→parenthetical notes when quoting legacy sources).
    19→
    20→| Old Name | New Name | Notes |
    21→|---|---|---|
    22→| Bardo | **Roko** | Overall framework/project name. "Bardo" was the umbrella name for the original ecosystem. |
    23→| Mori | **Roko Orchestrator** | Build/coding orchestration. Often just "orchestrator." The original Mori was a 108K-LOC TypeScript/Rust application for coding agent orchestration. |
    24→| Golem / Golems | **Agent / Agents** | The autonomous entity. "Agent" is the generic term; "Roko agent" when disambiguation is needed. The framework is Roko; individual entities are agents. |
    25→| Grimoire | **Neuro** / `roko-neuro` / **NeuroStore** | Knowledge management subsystem. Persists insights, heuristics, warnings with tier-based decay. |
    26→| Styx | **Agent Mesh** / **Mesh** | P2P relay and permissioned subnets for inter-agent communication and knowledge sharing. |
    27→| GNOS (token) | **KORAI** (mainnet) / **DAEJI** (testnet) | Token names. KORAI is the mainnet token on the Korai chain. DAEJI is the testnet equivalent on the Daeji testnet. |
    28→| Korai | **Korai** | Dedicated EVM chain for agent coordination. Mainnet. |
    29→| Daeji | **Daeji** | Testnet for the Korai chain. |
    30→| Clade | **Collective** / **Mesh** | Groups of cooperating agents. **Never use "fleet"** — this was an incorrect interim rename. |
    31→| Signal (code) | **Engram** (docs) | The universal data type. In Rust code it is `Signal` (`roko-core`). In PRD docs it is "Engram." These are **the same concept** — see note below. |
    32→
    33→> **Signal ↔ Engram:** The Rust codebase uses `Signal` as the struct name for the
    34→> universal datum (defined in `crates/roko-core/src/signal.rs`). The PRD documentation
    35→> calls the same concept an **Engram**, emphasizing its neuroscience roots (Semon 1904).
    36→> Whenever you see `Signal` in code, read it as "Engram." Whenever you see "Engram" in
    37→> docs, the corresponding Rust type is `Signal`. The six verb traits (`Substrate`,
    38→> `Scorer`, `Gate`, `Router`, `Composer`, `Policy`) all operate on `Signal` / Engram.
    39→
    40→---
    41→
    42→## 2. Configuration Files
    43→
    44→| Old | New |
    45→|---|---|
    46→| `golem.toml` | `roko.toml` |
    47→
    48→---
    49→
    50→## 3. Crate Names
    51→
    52→| Old Crate | New Crate | Notes |
    53→|---|---|---|
    54→| All `golem-*` | `roko-*` | Mechanical rename. |
    55→| All `bardo-*` | `roko-*` | Mechanical rename. Note: `bardo-runtime` and `bardo-primitives` still use old names in the current codebase. Rename is planned. |
    56→| `mori-index` | `roko-index` | Code parsing, symbol graphs, HDC fingerprints. |
    57→| `mori-context` | `roko-compose` + `roko-index` | Context features moved to `roko-compose`; code intelligence moved to `roko-index`. |
    58→| `mori-mcp` | `roko-mcp-{stdio,github,slack,scripts}` | Split into per-transport MCP server crates. |
    59→| `bardo-terminal` | `roko-cli` | The terminal UI is in `roko-cli`, with a separate TUI scaffold. |
    60→| `roko-golem` | **DISSOLVED** | See Crate Dissolution section below. |
    61→
    62→---
    63→
    64→## 4. Crate Dissolution: `roko-golem`
    65→
    66→The `roko-golem` crate has been dissolved. Its subsystems are redistributed to standalone
    67→crates following the composability principle: any subsystem can pipe to any other through
    68→Engrams and the six Synapse traits. No umbrella crate is needed.
    69→
    70→| Subsystem | Original Location | New Location | Notes |
    71→|---|---|---|---|
    72→| Daimon (972 lines, fully implemented) | `roko-golem/daimon.rs` | `roko-daimon` | Full PAD vector implementation, behavioral states, somatic markers. |
    73→| Dreams (43 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/dreams.rs` | `roko-dreams` | Placeholder deleted; `roko-dreams` is the expanded implementation. |
    74→| Grimoire (44 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/grimoire.rs` | `roko-neuro` | Placeholder deleted; `roko-neuro` is the replacement with tier-based knowledge management. |
    75→| Chain Witness (43 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/chain_witness.rs` | `roko-chain` as `chain_witness` module | Moved. |
    76→| Mortality (44 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/mortality.rs` | **DELETED ENTIRELY** | No mortality system in the new architecture. Resource constraints (budget, confidence, time) replace mortality clocks. |
    77→| Hypnagogia (42 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/hypnagogia.rs` | `roko-dreams` as `hypnagogia` module | Moved to the Dreams crate. |
    78→| `ScaffoldEngine` trait | `roko-golem/lib.rs` | **DELETED** | Each subsystem defines its own trait. No umbrella needed. |
    79→| `GolemScaffold` aggregator | `roko-golem/lib.rs` | **DELETED** | Composition happens at the application layer via configuration. |
    80→
    81→After dissolution, `roko-golem` is removed from workspace members in `Cargo.toml`.
    82→
    83→**Composability principle**: Any subsystem can pipe to any other. Daimon emits Engrams →
    84→Neuro stores them. Dreams reads from Neuro → produces new Engrams. Chain posts Engrams
    85→on-chain. Everything flows through the six Synapse traits.
    86→
    87→---
    88→
    89→## 5. Core Types
    90→
    91→| Old Name | New Name | Notes |
    92→|---|---|---|
    93→| `Signal` (as architectural noun) | **Engram** | The canonical architectural term. Used in all documentation. |
    94→| `Signal` (as Rust type name) | `Signal` (for now) | The Rust type is still named `Signal` in the current codebase. Rename to `Engram` is Tier 0D in the implementation plan. Documentation uses "Engram" with a note about the current code name. |
    95→| `SignalBuilder` | **EngramBuilder** | Builder pattern for Engram construction. Code currently uses `SignalBuilder`. |
    96→| `signal.rs` | `engram.rs` | Source file rename. |
    97→| "1 noun, 6 verbs" | **Synapse Architecture** | Architecture branding. The six traits are the "synapses" through which Engrams flow. |
    98→
    99→---
   100→
   101→## 6. Interface Names
   102→
   103→| Old | New |
   104→|---|---|
   105→| Bardo Sanctum | **Roko Portal** (web dashboard) |
   106→| bardo-terminal / Bardo | **Roko TUI** (terminal dashboard) |
   107→| Mori TUI | **Roko TUI** |
   108→
   109→---
   110→
   111→## 7. Token Details
   112→
   113→| Token | Chain | Notes |
   114→|---|---|---|
   115→| **KORAI** | Korai (mainnet) | 1% annual demurrage. Replaces GNOS. |
   116→| **DAEJI** | Daeji (testnet) | Testnet equivalent of KORAI. |
   117→
   118→When a legacy document mentions "GNOS token," the correct new name is "KORAI token" (or
   119→"DAEJI token" if explicitly about testnet). When it mentions "golem chain," the correct
   120→new name is "Korai chain."
   121→
   122→---
   123→
   124→## 8. Subsystem Names — Kept Unchanged
   125→
   126→These names are preserved from the original architecture with no rename needed:
   127→
   128→| Name | What It Is |
   129→|---|---|
   130→| **Mirage** / **mirage-rs** | In-process EVM simulator for transaction testing. |
   131→| **Heartbeat** | The agent's cognitive loop — one complete cycle of perceive → evaluate → attend → integrate → act → verify → persist → adapt → meta-cognize. |
   132→| **CoALA** | Cognitive Architecture for Language Agents (Sumers et al. 2023). The theoretical framework underlying the universal cognitive loop. |
   133→| **Pheromone system** | Stigmergic coordination: agents leave decaying signals in shared substrate for indirect communication (Grassé 1959, Parunak et al. 2007). |
   134→| **Sleepwalker** | Reduced-capability sleep mode for agents during low-activity periods. |
   135→| **Oneirography** | Recording and analysis of Dreams cycle outputs. |
   136→| **Hypnagogia** | The transitional state between waking and sleeping cognition, implemented as a creative hypothesis generator. |
   137→| **ALMA** | Three-layer affect model (Gebhard 2005) informing the Daimon's emotional processing. |
   138→| **Somatic markers** | Damasio's somatic marker hypothesis (Damasio 1994): emotional signals from past experience that bias decision-making. |
   139→| **Library of Babel** | Cross-collective knowledge sharing — named after Borges' infinite library concept. |
   140→| **Bazaar** | Commerce primitives for agent-to-agent economic interaction. |
   141→| **MPP** | Machine Payment Protocol — protocol for agent-initiated micropayments. |
   142→| **Testament** | Knowledge transfer between agents (repurposed from "death inheritance" to "knowledge export/import"). |
   143→| **Portal** | Interface concept for user interaction surfaces. |
   144→| **Spectre** | The procedurally generated creature visualization — a dot-cloud entity whose body encodes cognitive state. |
   145→| **ROSEDUST** | Dark-only design language (rose on void-black). The visual identity system for all Roko interfaces. |
   146→
   147→---
   148→
   149→## 9. New Names (Not in Legacy Sources)
   150→
   151→These terms are introduced in the new architecture and do not appear in legacy documents:
   152→
   153→| Term | Definition |
   154→|---|---|
   155→| **Engram** | The core data type — a content-addressed, scored, decaying, lineage-tracked unit of cognition. Replaces "Signal" as the architectural noun. |
   156→| **Synapse Architecture** | The 6-trait composition model. Replaces "1 noun, 6 verbs." |
   157→| **Five Layers** | Runtime (L0) / Framework (L1) / Scaffold (L2) / Harness (L3) / Orchestration (L4). |
   158→| **Cognitive Cross-Cuts** | Neuro / Daimon / Dreams — subsystems injected across multiple layers. |
   159→| **C-Factor** | Collective intelligence ratio metric (Woolley et al. 2010, Science 330). |
   160→| **C-Score** | Composite optimization metric for collective performance. |
   161→| **Three Cognitive Speeds** | Gamma (~5-15s reactive) / Theta (~75s reflective) / Delta (hours consolidation). |
   162→| **16 T0 Probes** | Zero-LLM diagnostic probes that suppress ~80% of ticks to T0 (no inference call). |
   163→| **VCG Attention Auction** | Vickrey-Clarke-Groves mechanism for truthful context budget allocation. |
   164→| **Somatic Landscape** | k-d tree over 8D strategy space with 15% contrarian retrieval for diversity. |
   165→| **Hypnagogia Engine** | Creative hypothesis generator: Thalamic Gate + Executive Loosener + Dali Interrupt + Homuncular Observer. |
   166→| **Cognitive Kernel Primitives** | Namespaces, cognitive signals, scheduling, syscalls — the "operating system" layer. |
   167→| **Korai Passport** | ERC-721 soulbound agent identity on the Korai chain. |
   168→| **Spore / Sparrow** | Job market protocols for agent task discovery and claiming. |
   169→| **ISFR** | Intersubjective Fact Registry — decentralized fact verification. |
   170→| **Valhalla** | Privacy layer using TEE, PSI (Private Set Intersection), and ZK proofs. |
   171→
   172→---
   173→
   174→## 10. Glossary of Architectural Terms
   175→
   176→### A
   177→
   178→| Term | Definition |
   179→|---|---|
   180→| **A2A** | Agent-to-Agent protocol. Google's open standard for inter-agent communication. |
   181→| **A2UI** | Agent-to-User-Interface. Agents generate their own UI components in ROSEDUST. |
   182→| **Active Inference** | Framework from neuroscience (Friston 2010): self-organizing systems minimize prediction error through perception and action. Roko uses this as a conceptual framework for attention and tier routing. |
   183→| **ADAS** | Automated Design of Agentic Systems (Hu et al. ICLR 2025). Meta-agent that searches the space of agent architectures. |
   184→| **Adaptive Clock** | The scheduler that manages three cognitive speeds (Gamma/Theta/Delta) and adjusts cadence based on affect state and task characteristics. |
   185→| **Agent Mesh** | P2P communication and knowledge sharing between Roko agents. Replaces "Styx." |
   186→| **Attestation** | Cryptographic proof of Engram origin — Ed25519 signature with optional chain attestation. |
   187→
   188→### B
   189→
   190→| Term | Definition |
   191→|---|---|
   192→| **Body** | The typed payload of an Engram. Variants: Empty (marker), Text (UTF-8), Json (structured), Bytes (binary). |
   193→| **Budget** | Resource constraints for Composer operations: max_tokens, max_signals, max_bytes, max_wall_ms. |
   194→
   195→### C
   196→
   197→| Term | Definition |
   198→|---|---|
   199→| **C-Factor** | Collective intelligence ratio: `C-Factor = Collective Performance / Sum(Individual Performances)`. Values > 1.0 indicate superlinear intelligence. (Woolley et al. 2010) |
   200→| **C-Score** | Composite optimization metric: `gate_pass×0.3 + cost_efficiency×0.2 + speed×0.15 + first_try_rate×0.25 + knowledge_growth×0.1`. |
   201→| **CascadeRouter** | Multi-stage model routing: confidence threshold → contextual bandit (LinUCB) → cost-aware selection. Persisted to `.roko/learn/cascade-router.json`. |
   202→| **Cognitive Loop** | The 9-step universal loop: PERCEIVE → EVALUATE → ATTEND → INTEGRATE → ACT → VERIFY → PERSIST → ADAPT → META-COGNIZE. |
   203→| **Collective** | A group of cooperating agents. Replaces "Clade." |
   204→| **Composer** | Synapse trait. Combines multiple Engrams into one new Engram under Budget constraints. Synchronous. Takes `&dyn Scorer`. |
   205→| **ContentHash** | 32-byte BLAKE3 digest identifying an Engram. Computed from kind + body + author + tags. Score and decay are excluded. |
   206→| **Context** | The shared runtime environment passed to every trait method. Carries time (`now_ms`), goal, session, and extension attributes. |
   207→
   208→### D
   209→
   210→| Term | Definition |
   211→|---|---|
   212→| **Daimon** | Motivation and focus subsystem (`roko-daimon`). Maintains PAD (Pleasure-Arousal-Dominance) vectors, six behavioral states, and somatic markers. The agent's self-model. |
   213→| **Decay** | Time-based weight reduction for Engrams. Variants: None (permanent), HalfLife (exponential), Ttl (hard cutoff), Ebbinghaus (psychological forgetting curve). |
   214→| **Delta** | Consolidation cognitive speed. Hours timescale. Dreams: replay, synthesis, pruning. Knowledge tier promotion. |
   215→| **Dreams** | Offline learning subsystem (`roko-dreams`). Three-phase cycle: NREM replay + REM imagination + integration staging. |
   216→| **Dual-Process** | Kahneman's System 1/System 2 mapped to Roko: T0 (direct tool call, no LLM) / T1 (fast model) / T2 (full model, deep reasoning). |
   217→
   218→### E
   219→
   220→| Term | Definition |
   221→|---|---|
   222→| **EFE** | Expected Free Energy. `G(π) = −Pragmatic Value − Epistemic Value`. Drives tier routing without hyperparameters. |
   223→| **Engram** | The universal data type of Roko. Content-addressed (BLAKE3), scored (7-axis), decaying, lineage-tracked. Every piece of information in the system is an Engram. Currently named `Signal` in code; rename is Tier 0D. |
   224→| **EngramBuilder** | Builder pattern for constructing Engrams. Currently named `SignalBuilder` in code. |
   225→| **Episode** | A recorded sequence of agent actions and their outcomes. Stored as Engrams with `Kind::Episode`. |
   226→| **EvoSkills** | Self-evolving skill libraries via adversarial surrogate verification (Zhao et al. 2024). |
   227→
   228→### F-G
   229→
   230→| Term | Definition |
   231→|---|---|
   232→| **Forensic AI** | Content-addressed causal replay — the ability to trace any decision back to its inputs via lineage chains. Regulatory pre-compliance moat. |
   233→| **Gamma** | Reactive cognitive speed. ~5-15 second timescale. One complete loop tick: tool calls, LLM inference, verification. |
   234→| **Gate** | Synapse trait. Verifies an Engram against ground truth, producing a Verdict. Asynchronous. The bridge to external reality. |
   235→
   236→### H-K
   237→
   238→| Term | Definition |
   239→|---|---|
   240→| **HDC** | Hyperdimensional Computing. 10,240-bit vectors using XOR bind, majority bundle, Hamming similarity. Used for semantic similarity in knowledge retrieval. (Kanerva 2009, Cognitive Computation 1(2); Plate 2003; Frady et al. 2018) |
   241→| **Hypnagogia** | The creative hypothesis generator in `roko-dreams`. Four components: Thalamic Gate (stimulus filter), Executive Loosener (constraint relaxation), Dali Interrupt (capture mechanism), Homuncular Observer (coherence filter). |
   242→| **Kind** | The semantic type of an Engram. `#[non_exhaustive]` enum with variants for agent runtime, verification, tasks, context, routing, memory, observability, chain participation, and `Custom(String)` for extensions. |
   243→| **KORAI** | Mainnet token on the Korai chain. 1% annual demurrage. Replaces "GNOS." |
   244→| **Korai** | Dedicated EVM chain for agent coordination. |
   245→
   246→### L-N
   247→
   248→| Term | Definition |
   249→|---|---|
   250→| **Lineage** | A vector of ContentHashes tracking the parent Engrams from which a new Engram was derived. Forms an audit DAG. |
   251→| **Neuro** | Knowledge management subsystem (`roko-neuro`). Six knowledge types (Insight, Heuristic, Warning, CausalLink, StrategyFragment, AntiKnowledge). Four tiers (Transient, Working, Consolidated, Persistent). HDC encoding for similarity. |
   252→| **NeuroStore** | The storage backend for the Neuro subsystem. |
   253→
   254→### O-P
   255→
   256→| Term | Definition |
   257→|---|---|
   258→| **Outcome** | Feedback about a prior Router selection. Carries success/failure, reward, cost, and latency. Used by Routers to learn via bandit algorithms. |
   259→| **Pheromone** | A stigmergic signal with time-based decay. Three types: THREAT (2h half-life), OPPORTUNITY (4h), WISDOM (24h). Agents communicate indirectly by reading and writing pheromones to shared Substrates. |
   260→| **Policy** | Synapse trait. Watches Engram streams and emits new Engrams in response (interventions, reactions). Synchronous. Batch input. |
   261→| **Provenance** | Who produced an Engram and how trustworthy they are. Fields: author (String), trust ([0,1]), tainted (bool), session (Option). |
   262→
   263→### Q-R
   264→
   265→| Term | Definition |
   266→|---|---|
   267→| **Query** | Filter specification for Substrate lookups. Fields: kinds, author, session, since_ms, until_ms, min_weight, tags, limit. |
   268→| **ROSEDUST** | Dark-only design system. Rose on void-black. The visual identity for all Roko interfaces. |
   269→| **Router** | Synapse trait. Selects one Engram from candidates. Has `feedback()` method for learning from outcomes. Synchronous. |
   270→
   271→### S
   272→
   273→| Term | Definition |
   274→|---|---|
   275→| **Score** | Multi-dimensional quality assessment of an Engram. 4 stable axes: confidence [0,1], novelty [0,1], utility [0,∞), reputation [0,∞). 3 extended axes (planned): precision [0,1], salience [0,1], coherence [0,1]. Effective score formula: `confidence × (1 + novelty) × (1 + utility) × reputation`. |
   276→| **Scorer** | Synapse trait. Rates an Engram along multiple axes, producing a Score. Synchronous. Pure function of (Engram, Context). |
   277→| **Selection** | Output of a Router: the chosen Engram's ContentHash, confidence, router name, and optional reasoning. |
   278→| **Spectre** | Procedurally generated dot-cloud creature per agent. Body encodes cognitive state; eyes encode emotion; clarity encodes prediction accuracy. |
   279→| **Substrate** | Synapse trait. Stores and queries Engrams. Asynchronous. Multiple backends: MemorySubstrate (testing), FileSubstrate (JSONL persistence), HdcSubstrate (semantic search), ChainSubstrate (on-chain state). |
   280→| **Synapse Architecture** | The compositional model underlying Roko: one noun (Engram) + six verb traits (Substrate, Scorer, Gate, Router, Composer, Policy). |
   281→
   282→### T-V
   283→
   284→| Term | Definition |
   285→|---|---|
   286→| **T0 / T1 / T2** | Inference tiers. T0: no LLM call (direct tool call, ~80% of ticks). T1: fast model, shallow reasoning (~15%). T2: full model, deep reasoning (~5%). Routing emerges from active inference (EFE). |
   287→| **Theta** | Reflective cognitive speed. ~75 second timescale. Summarize recent work, update Daimon state, check predictions. |
   288→| **TickOutcome** | The result of one `loop_tick` invocation. Contains: candidates_examined, composed Engram, Verdict, emitted Engrams, written ContentHashes. |
   289→| **Verdict** | Output of a Gate. Contains: passed (bool), reason (String), gate name, score [0,1], optional detail, optional TestCount, optional error_digest, duration_ms. |
   290→
   291→---
   292→
   293→## 11. Naming Application Rules
   294→
   295→1. **When quoting legacy sources verbatim**: Keep the old name in the quote but add a
   296→   parenthetical: "(formerly Grimoire, now Neuro)."
   297→2. **When paraphrasing**: Use the new name directly.
   298→3. **Code samples**: Use new crate names (`roko-primitives`, `roko-runtime`, etc.) even if
   299→   the current Rust code still uses old names. Note the current name in a comment.
   300→4. **Struct/type names**: Say "Engram" in prose. In Rust code snippets, use `Signal` and
   301→   add a comment like `// will be renamed to Engram in Tier 0D`.
   302→5. **File paths**: All `bardo-*` → `roko-*`, all `mori-*` → `roko-*`.
   303→6. **Never say**: "Golem SDK" → say "Agent SDK" or "Roko SDK".
   304→7. **Never say**: "Mori + Golem" → say "Roko framework with coding and chain domain plugins."
   305→
   306→---
   307→
   308→## Academic Foundations
   309→
   310→| Citation | Relevance |
   311→|---|---|
   312→| Woolley et al. 2010, Science 330(6004) | C-Factor: collective intelligence metric. |
   313→| Sumers et al. 2023 (arXiv:2309.02427) | CoALA: cognitive architecture framework. |
   314→| Friston 2010, Nature Reviews Neuroscience 11(2) | Free Energy Principle, active inference. |
   315→| Kanerva 2009, Cognitive Computation 1(2) | Hyperdimensional Computing foundations. |
   316→| Grassé 1959 | Stigmergy: indirect coordination via environmental signals. |
   317→| Damasio 1994, Descartes' Error | Somatic markers: emotion biasing decision-making. |
   318→| Mehrabian & Russell 1974 | PAD model: Pleasure-Arousal-Dominance emotional space. |
   319→| Gebhard 2005 | ALMA: three-layer affect model. |
   320→| Kahneman 2011, Thinking, Fast and Slow | Dual-process theory: System 1 / System 2. |
   321→
   322→---
   323→
   324→## Cross-References
   325→
   326→- [00-vision-and-thesis.md](00-vision-and-thesis.md) — Why the architecture exists
   327→- [02-engram-data-type.md](02-engram-data-type.md) — Full Engram specification
   328→- [03-score-7-axis-appraisal.md](03-score-7-axis-appraisal.md) — Score details
   329→- [06-synapse-traits.md](06-synapse-traits.md) — The six Synapse traits
   330→

file.content:
# Naming Map and Glossary

> **Abstract:** This document provides the authoritative naming map for all Roko
> terminology, mapping legacy names to their current equivalents and defining every
> Roko-specific term, abbreviation, and architectural concept used across the PRD suite.
> When any other document disagrees with a definition here, this glossary is authoritative.
> This serves as a quick-reference companion to the full architecture documentation.


> **Implementation**: Shipping

---

## 1. Project and Framework Names

The Roko project has undergone several naming iterations. The following table is the
canonical old-to-new mapping. All documentation uses the new names exclusively (with
parenthetical notes when quoting legacy sources).

| Old Name | New Name | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Bardo | **Roko** | Overall framework/project name. "Bardo" was the umbrella name for the original ecosystem. |
| Mori | **Roko Orchestrator** | Build/coding orchestration. Often just "orchestrator." The original Mori was a 108K-LOC TypeScript/Rust application for coding agent orchestration. |
| Golem / Golems | **Agent / Agents** | The autonomous entity. "Agent" is the generic term; "Roko agent" when disambiguation is needed. The framework is Roko; individual entities are agents. |
| Grimoire | **Neuro** / `roko-neuro` / **NeuroStore** | Knowledge management subsystem. Persists insights, heuristics, warnings with tier-based decay. |
| Styx | **Agent Mesh** / **Mesh** | P2P relay and permissioned subnets for inter-agent communication and knowledge sharing. |
| GNOS (token) | **KORAI** (mainnet) / **DAEJI** (testnet) | Token names. KORAI is the mainnet token on the Korai chain. DAEJI is the testnet equivalent on the Daeji testnet. |
| Korai | **Korai** | Dedicated EVM chain for agent coordination. Mainnet. |
| Daeji | **Daeji** | Testnet for the Korai chain. |
| Clade | **Collective** / **Mesh** | Groups of cooperating agents. **Never use "fleet"** — this was an incorrect interim rename. |
| Signal (code) | **Engram** (docs) | The universal data type. In Rust code it is `Signal` (`roko-core`). In PRD docs it is "Engram." These are **the same concept** — see note below. |

> **Signal ↔ Engram:** The Rust codebase uses `Signal` as the struct name for the
> universal datum (defined in `crates/roko-core/src/signal.rs`). The PRD documentation
> calls the same concept an **Engram**, emphasizing its neuroscience roots (Semon 1904).
> Whenever you see `Signal` in code, read it as "Engram." Whenever you see "Engram" in
> docs, the corresponding Rust type is `Signal`. The six verb traits (`Substrate`,
> `Scorer`, `Gate`, `Router`, `Composer`, `Policy`) all operate on `Signal` / Engram.

---

## 2. Configuration Files

| Old | New |
|---|---|
| `golem.toml` | `roko.toml` |

---

## 3. Crate Names

| Old Crate | New Crate | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| All `golem-*` | `roko-*` | Mechanical rename. |
| All `bardo-*` | `roko-*` | Mechanical rename. Note: `bardo-runtime` and `bardo-primitives` still use old names in the current codebase. Rename is planned. |
| `mori-index` | `roko-index` | Code parsing, symbol graphs, HDC fingerprints. |
| `mori-context` | `roko-compose` + `roko-index` | Context features moved to `roko-compose`; code intelligence moved to `roko-index`. |
| `mori-mcp` | `roko-mcp-{stdio,github,slack,scripts}` | Split into per-transport MCP server crates. |
| `bardo-terminal` | `roko-cli` | The terminal UI is in `roko-cli`, with a separate TUI scaffold. |
| `roko-golem` | **DISSOLVED** | See Crate Dissolution section below. |

---

## 4. Crate Dissolution: `roko-golem`

The `roko-golem` crate has been dissolved. Its subsystems are redistributed to standalone
crates following the composability principle: any subsystem can pipe to any other through
Engrams and the six Synapse traits. No umbrella crate is needed.

| Subsystem | Original Location | New Location | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Daimon (972 lines, fully implemented) | `roko-golem/daimon.rs` | `roko-daimon` | Full PAD vector implementation, behavioral states, somatic markers. |
| Dreams (43 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/dreams.rs` | `roko-dreams` | Placeholder deleted; `roko-dreams` is the expanded implementation. |
| Grimoire (44 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/grimoire.rs` | `roko-neuro` | Placeholder deleted; `roko-neuro` is the replacement with tier-based knowledge management. |
| Chain Witness (43 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/chain_witness.rs` | `roko-chain` as `chain_witness` module | Moved. |
| Mortality (44 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/mortality.rs` | **DELETED ENTIRELY** | No mortality system in the new architecture. Resource constraints (budget, confidence, time) replace mortality clocks. |
| Hypnagogia (42 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/hypnagogia.rs` | `roko-dreams` as `hypnagogia` module | Moved to the Dreams crate. |
| `ScaffoldEngine` trait | `roko-golem/lib.rs` | **DELETED** | Each subsystem defines its own trait. No umbrella needed. |
| `GolemScaffold` aggregator | `roko-golem/lib.rs` | **DELETED** | Composition happens at the application layer via configuration. |

After dissolution, `roko-golem` is removed from workspace members in `Cargo.toml`.

**Composability principle**: Any subsystem can pipe to any other. Daimon emits Engrams →
Neuro stores them. Dreams reads from Neuro → produces new Engrams. Chain posts Engrams
on-chain. Everything flows through the six Synapse traits.

---

## 5. Core Types

| Old Name | New Name | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `Signal` (as architectural noun) | **Engram** | The canonical architectural term. Used in all documentation. |
| `Signal` (as Rust type name) | `Signal` (for now) | The Rust type is still named `Signal` in the current codebase. Rename to `Engram` is Tier 0D in the implementation plan. Documentation uses "Engram" with a note about the current code name. |
| `SignalBuilder` | **EngramBuilder** | Builder pattern for Engram construction. Code currently uses `SignalBuilder`. |
| `signal.rs` | `engram.rs` | Source file rename. |
| "1 noun, 6 verbs" | **Synapse Architecture** | Architecture branding. The six traits are the "synapses" through which Engrams flow. |

---

## 6. Interface Names

| Old | New |
|---|---|
| Bardo Sanctum | **Roko Portal** (web dashboard) |
| bardo-terminal / Bardo | **Roko TUI** (terminal dashboard) |
| Mori TUI | **Roko TUI** |

---

## 7. Token Details

| Token | Chain | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **KORAI** | Korai (mainnet) | 1% annual demurrage. Replaces GNOS. |
| **DAEJI** | Daeji (testnet) | Testnet equivalent of KORAI. |

When a legacy document mentions "GNOS token," the correct new name is "KORAI token" (or
"DAEJI token" if explicitly about testnet). When it mentions "golem chain," the correct
new name is "Korai chain."

---

## 8. Subsystem Names — Kept Unchanged

These names are preserved from the original architecture with no rename needed:

| Name | What It Is |
|---|---|
| **Mirage** / **mirage-rs** | In-process EVM simulator for transaction testing. |
| **Heartbeat** | The agent's cognitive loop — one complete cycle of perceive → evaluate → attend → integrate → act → verify → persist → adapt → meta-cognize. |
| **CoALA** | Cognitive Architecture for Language Agents (Sumers et al. 2023). The theoretical framework underlying the universal cognitive loop. |
| **Pheromone system** | Stigmergic coordination: agents leave decaying signals in shared substrate for indirect communication (Grassé 1959, Parunak et al. 2007). |
| **Sleepwalker** | Reduced-capability sleep mode for agents during low-activity periods. |
| **Oneirography** | Recording and analysis of Dreams cycle outputs. |
| **Hypnagogia** | The transitional state between waking and sleeping cognition, implemented as a creative hypothesis generator. |
| **ALMA** | Three-layer affect model (Gebhard 2005) informing the Daimon's emotional processing. |
| **Somatic markers** | Damasio's somatic marker hypothesis (Damasio 1994): emotional signals from past experience that bias decision-making. |
| **Library of Babel** | Cross-collective knowledge sharing — named after Borges' infinite library concept. |
| **Bazaar** | Commerce primitives for agent-to-agent economic interaction. |
| **MPP** | Machine Payment Protocol — protocol for agent-initiated micropayments. |
| **Testament** | Knowledge transfer between agents (repurposed from "death inheritance" to "knowledge export/import"). |
| **Portal** | Interface concept for user interaction surfaces. |
| **Spectre** | The procedurally generated creature visualization — a dot-cloud entity whose body encodes cognitive state. |
| **ROSEDUST** | Dark-only design language (rose on void-black). The visual identity system for all Roko interfaces. |

---

## 9. New Names (Not in Legacy Sources)

These terms are introduced in the new architecture and do not appear in legacy documents:

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Engram** | The core data type — a content-addressed, scored, decaying, lineage-tracked unit of cognition. Replaces "Signal" as the architectural noun. |
| **Synapse Architecture** | The 6-trait composition model. Replaces "1 noun, 6 verbs." |
| **Five Layers** | Runtime (L0) / Framework (L1) / Scaffold (L2) / Harness (L3) / Orchestration (L4). |
| **Cognitive Cross-Cuts** | Neuro / Daimon / Dreams — subsystems injected across multiple layers. |
| **C-Factor** | Collective intelligence ratio metric (Woolley et al. 2010, Science 330). |
| **C-Score** | Composite optimization metric for collective performance. |
| **Three Cognitive Speeds** | Gamma (~5-15s reactive) / Theta (~75s reflective) / Delta (hours consolidation). |
| **16 T0 Probes** | Zero-LLM diagnostic probes that suppress ~80% of ticks to T0 (no inference call). |
| **VCG Attention Auction** | Vickrey-Clarke-Groves mechanism for truthful context budget allocation. |
| **Somatic Landscape** | k-d tree over 8D strategy space with 15% contrarian retrieval for diversity. |
| **Hypnagogia Engine** | Creative hypothesis generator: Thalamic Gate + Executive Loosener + Dali Interrupt + Homuncular Observer. |
| **Cognitive Kernel Primitives** | Namespaces, cognitive signals, scheduling, syscalls — the "operating system" layer. |
| **Korai Passport** | ERC-721 soulbound agent identity on the Korai chain. |
| **Spore / Sparrow** | Job market protocols for agent task discovery and claiming. |
| **ISFR** | Intersubjective Fact Registry — decentralized fact verification. |
| **Valhalla** | Privacy layer using TEE, PSI (Private Set Intersection), and ZK proofs. |

---

## 10. Glossary of Architectural Terms

### A

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **A2A** | Agent-to-Agent protocol. Google's open standard for inter-agent communication. |
| **A2UI** | Agent-to-User-Interface. Agents generate their own UI components in ROSEDUST. |
| **Active Inference** | Framework from neuroscience (Friston 2010): self-organizing systems minimize prediction error through perception and action. Roko uses this as a conceptual framework for attention and tier routing. |
| **ADAS** | Automated Design of Agentic Systems (Hu et al. ICLR 2025). Meta-agent that searches the space of agent architectures. |
| **Adaptive Clock** | The scheduler that manages three cognitive speeds (Gamma/Theta/Delta) and adjusts cadence based on affect state and task characteristics. |
| **Agent Mesh** | P2P communication and knowledge sharing between Roko agents. Replaces "Styx." |
| **Attestation** | Cryptographic proof of Engram origin — Ed25519 signature with optional chain attestation. |

### B

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Body** | The typed payload of an Engram. Variants: Empty (marker), Text (UTF-8), Json (structured), Bytes (binary). |
| **Budget** | Resource constraints for Composer operations: max_tokens, max_signals, max_bytes, max_wall_ms. |

### C

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **C-Factor** | Collective intelligence ratio: `C-Factor = Collective Performance / Sum(Individual Performances)`. Values > 1.0 indicate superlinear intelligence. (Woolley et al. 2010) |
| **C-Score** | Composite optimization metric: `gate_pass×0.3 + cost_efficiency×0.2 + speed×0.15 + first_try_rate×0.25 + knowledge_growth×0.1`. |
| **CascadeRouter** | Multi-stage model routing: confidence threshold → contextual bandit (LinUCB) → cost-aware selection. Persisted to `.roko/learn/cascade-router.json`. |
| **Cognitive Loop** | The 9-step universal loop: PERCEIVE → EVALUATE → ATTEND → INTEGRATE → ACT → VERIFY → PERSIST → ADAPT → META-COGNIZE. |
| **Collective** | A group of cooperating agents. Replaces "Clade." |
| **Composer** | Synapse trait. Combines multiple Engrams into one new Engram under Budget constraints. Synchronous. Takes `&dyn Scorer`. |
| **ContentHash** | 32-byte BLAKE3 digest identifying an Engram. Computed from kind + body + author + tags. Score and decay are excluded. |
| **Context** | The shared runtime environment passed to every trait method. Carries time (`now_ms`), goal, session, and extension attributes. |

### D

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Daimon** | Motivation and focus subsystem (`roko-daimon`). Maintains PAD (Pleasure-Arousal-Dominance) vectors, six behavioral states, and somatic markers. The agent's self-model. |
| **Decay** | Time-based weight reduction for Engrams. Variants: None (permanent), HalfLife (exponential), Ttl (hard cutoff), Ebbinghaus (psychological forgetting curve). |
| **Delta** | Consolidation cognitive speed. Hours timescale. Dreams: replay, synthesis, pruning. Knowledge tier promotion. |
| **Dreams** | Offline learning subsystem (`roko-dreams`). Three-phase cycle: NREM replay + REM imagination + integration staging. |
| **Dual-Process** | Kahneman's System 1/System 2 mapped to Roko: T0 (direct tool call, no LLM) / T1 (fast model) / T2 (full model, deep reasoning). |

### E

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **EFE** | Expected Free Energy. `G(π) = −Pragmatic Value − Epistemic Value`. Drives tier routing without hyperparameters. |
| **Engram** | The universal data type of Roko. Content-addressed (BLAKE3), scored (7-axis), decaying, lineage-tracked. Every piece of information in the system is an Engram. Currently named `Signal` in code; rename is Tier 0D. |
| **EngramBuilder** | Builder pattern for constructing Engrams. Currently named `SignalBuilder` in code. |
| **Episode** | A recorded sequence of agent actions and their outcomes. Stored as Engrams with `Kind::Episode`. |
| **EvoSkills** | Self-evolving skill libraries via adversarial surrogate verification (Zhao et al. 2024). |

### F-G

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Forensic AI** | Content-addressed causal replay — the ability to trace any decision back to its inputs via lineage chains. Regulatory pre-compliance moat. |
| **Gamma** | Reactive cognitive speed. ~5-15 second timescale. One complete loop tick: tool calls, LLM inference, verification. |
| **Gate** | Synapse trait. Verifies an Engram against ground truth, producing a Verdict. Asynchronous. The bridge to external reality. |

### H-K

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **HDC** | Hyperdimensional Computing. 10,240-bit vectors using XOR bind, majority bundle, Hamming similarity. Used for semantic similarity in knowledge retrieval. (Kanerva 2009, Cognitive Computation 1(2); Plate 2003; Frady et al. 2018) |
| **Hypnagogia** | The creative hypothesis generator in `roko-dreams`. Four components: Thalamic Gate (stimulus filter), Executive Loosener (constraint relaxation), Dali Interrupt (capture mechanism), Homuncular Observer (coherence filter). |
| **Kind** | The semantic type of an Engram. `#[non_exhaustive]` enum with variants for agent runtime, verification, tasks, context, routing, memory, observability, chain participation, and `Custom(String)` for extensions. |
| **KORAI** | Mainnet token on the Korai chain. 1% annual demurrage. Replaces "GNOS." |
| **Korai** | Dedicated EVM chain for agent coordination. |

### L-N

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Lineage** | A vector of ContentHashes tracking the parent Engrams from which a new Engram was derived. Forms an audit DAG. |
| **Neuro** | Knowledge management subsystem (`roko-neuro`). Six knowledge types (Insight, Heuristic, Warning, CausalLink, StrategyFragment, AntiKnowledge). Four tiers (Transient, Working, Consolidated, Persistent). HDC encoding for similarity. |
| **NeuroStore** | The storage backend for the Neuro subsystem. |

### O-P

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Outcome** | Feedback about a prior Router selection. Carries success/failure, reward, cost, and latency. Used by Routers to learn via bandit algorithms. |
| **Pheromone** | A stigmergic signal with time-based decay. Three types: THREAT (2h half-life), OPPORTUNITY (4h), WISDOM (24h). Agents communicate indirectly by reading and writing pheromones to shared Substrates. |
| **Policy** | Synapse trait. Watches Engram streams and emits new Engrams in response (interventions, reactions). Synchronous. Batch input. |
| **Provenance** | Who produced an Engram and how trustworthy they are. Fields: author (String), trust ([0,1]), tainted (bool), session (Option). |

### Q-R

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Query** | Filter specification for Substrate lookups. Fields: kinds, author, session, since_ms, until_ms, min_weight, tags, limit. |
| **ROSEDUST** | Dark-only design system. Rose on void-black. The visual identity for all Roko interfaces. |
| **Router** | Synapse trait. Selects one Engram from candidates. Has `feedback()` method for learning from outcomes. Synchronous. |

### S

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Score** | Multi-dimensional quality assessment of an Engram. 4 stable axes: confidence [0,1], novelty [0,1], utility [0,∞), reputation [0,∞). 3 extended axes (planned): precision [0,1], salience [0,1], coherence [0,1]. Effective score formula: `confidence × (1 + novelty) × (1 + utility) × reputation`. |
| **Scorer** | Synapse trait. Rates an Engram along multiple axes, producing a Score. Synchronous. Pure function of (Engram, Context). |
| **Selection** | Output of a Router: the chosen Engram's ContentHash, confidence, router name, and optional reasoning. |
| **Spectre** | Procedurally generated dot-cloud creature per agent. Body encodes cognitive state; eyes encode emotion; clarity encodes prediction accuracy. |
| **Substrate** | Synapse trait. Stores and queries Engrams. Asynchronous. Multiple backends: MemorySubstrate (testing), FileSubstrate (JSONL persistence), HdcSubstrate (semantic search), ChainSubstrate (on-chain state). |
| **Synapse Architecture** | The compositional model underlying Roko: one noun (Engram) + six verb traits (Substrate, Scorer, Gate, Router, Composer, Policy). |

### T-V

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **T0 / T1 / T2** | Inference tiers. T0: no LLM call (direct tool call, ~80% of ticks). T1: fast model, shallow reasoning (~15%). T2: full model, deep reasoning (~5%). Routing emerges from active inference (EFE). |
| **Theta** | Reflective cognitive speed. ~75 second timescale. Summarize recent work, update Daimon state, check predictions. |
| **TickOutcome** | The result of one `loop_tick` invocation. Contains: candidates_examined, composed Engram, Verdict, emitted Engrams, written ContentHashes. |
| **Verdict** | Output of a Gate. Contains: passed (bool), reason (String), gate name, score [0,1], optional detail, optional TestCount, optional error_digest, duration_ms. |

---

## 11. Naming Application Rules

1. **When quoting legacy sources verbatim**: Keep the old name in the quote but add a
   parenthetical: "(formerly Grimoire, now Neuro)."
2. **When paraphrasing**: Use the new name directly.
3. **Code samples**: Use new crate names (`roko-primitives`, `roko-runtime`, etc.) even if
   the current Rust code still uses old names. Note the current name in a comment.
4. **Struct/type names**: Say "Engram" in prose. In Rust code snippets, use `Signal` and
   add a comment like `// will be renamed to Engram in Tier 0D`.
5. **File paths**: All `bardo-*` → `roko-*`, all `mori-*` → `roko-*`.
6. **Never say**: "Golem SDK" → say "Agent SDK" or "Roko SDK".
7. **Never say**: "Mori + Golem" → say "Roko framework with coding and chain domain plugins."

---

## Academic Foundations

| Citation | Relevance |
|---|---|
| Woolley et al. 2010, Science 330(6004) | C-Factor: collective intelligence metric. |
| Sumers et al. 2023 (arXiv:2309.02427) | CoALA: cognitive architecture framework. |
| Friston 2010, Nature Reviews Neuroscience 11(2) | Free Energy Principle, active inference. |
| Kanerva 2009, Cognitive Computation 1(2) | Hyperdimensional Computing foundations. |
| Grassé 1959 | Stigmergy: indirect coordination via environmental signals. |
| Damasio 1994, Descartes' Error | Somatic markers: emotion biasing decision-making. |
| Mehrabian & Russell 1974 | PAD model: Pleasure-Arousal-Dominance emotional space. |
| Gebhard 2005 | ALMA: three-layer affect model. |
| Kahneman 2011, Thinking, Fast and Slow | Dual-process theory: System 1 / System 2. |

---

## Cross-References

- [00-vision-and-thesis.md](00-vision-and-thesis.md) — Why the architecture exists
- [02-engram-data-type.md](02-engram-data-type.md) — Full Engram specification
- [03-score-7-axis-appraisal.md](03-score-7-axis-appraisal.md) — Score details
- [06-synapse-traits.md](06-synapse-traits.md) — The six Synapse traits

# Roko PRD — Master Index

> Generated by the PRD migration runner on 2026-04-12T02:32:51+02:00.
> Run ID: `run-20260412-023250`

This is the top-level index for the Roko PRD documentation. Each topic below
has its own folder with multiple sub-docs. For naming conventions, reframe rules,
and the authoritative source of truth, see `tmp/prd-migration/README.md` and
`/Users/will/dev/nunchi/roko/refactoring-prd/`.

## Start Here

- **[QUICKSTART.md](QUICKSTART.md)** — 4-level graduated onboarding (60 seconds → deep dive)
- **[STATUS.md](STATUS.md)** — Implementation status matrix (what's shipping vs. specified)
- **[COMPARISON.md](COMPARISON.md)** — How Roko compares to LangChain, CrewAI, SWE-Agent, AutoGPT

## Signal / Engram Naming

> **Code ↔ Docs:** The universal data type is called **`Signal`** in the Rust codebase
> (`crates/roko-core/src/signal.rs`) and **Engram** in these PRD documents. They are the
> same concept. `Signal` = Engram. See [`00-architecture/01-naming-and-glossary.md`](00-architecture/01-naming-and-glossary.md)
> for the full naming map including all legacy-to-current renames.

## Topics

- [`00-architecture/`](00-architecture/INDEX.md) — Architecture
- [`01-orchestration/`](01-orchestration/INDEX.md) — 01-orchestration — L4 Orchestration Layer
- [`02-agents/`](02-agents/INDEX.md) — 02 — Agents
- [`03-composition/`](03-composition/INDEX.md) — 03 — Composition: Scaffold Layer (L2) — Prompt Assembly & Context Engineering
- [`04-verification/`](04-verification/INDEX.md) — 04 — Verification (L3 Harness)
- [`05-learning/`](05-learning/INDEX.md) — 05 — Learning
- [`06-neuro/`](06-neuro/INDEX.md) — Neuro — Cognitive Knowledge Layer
- [`07-conductor/`](07-conductor/INDEX.md) — 07 — Conductor Subsystem
- [`08-chain/`](08-chain/INDEX.md) — Topic 08: Chain Layer (Korai)
- [`09-daimon/`](09-daimon/INDEX.md) — Topic 09: Daimon — Affect Engine
- [`10-dreams/`](10-dreams/INDEX.md) — Dreams (Offline Learning and Consolidation)
- [`11-safety/`](11-safety/INDEX.md) — Safety & Provenance
- [`12-interfaces/`](12-interfaces/INDEX.md) — Topic 12: Interfaces
- [`13-coordination/`](13-coordination/INDEX.md) — Coordination: Stigmergy, Pheromones, and Collective Intelligence
- [`14-identity-economy/`](14-identity-economy/INDEX.md) — 14 — Identity & Economy Layer
- [`15-code-intelligence/`](15-code-intelligence/INDEX.md) — Code Intelligence
- [`16-heartbeat/`](16-heartbeat/INDEX.md) — Heartbeat: The Cognitive Clock
- [`17-lifecycle/`](17-lifecycle/INDEX.md) — Agent Lifecycle

content.content:
1→# Naming Map and Glossary
     2→
     3→> **Abstract:** This document provides the authoritative naming map for all Roko
     4→> terminology, mapping legacy names to their current equivalents and defining every
     5→> Roko-specific term, abbreviation, and architectural concept used across the PRD suite.
     6→> When any other document disagrees with a definition here, this glossary is authoritative.
     7→> This serves as a quick-reference companion to the full architecture documentation.
     8→
     9→
    10→> **Implementation**: Shipping
    11→
    12→---
    13→
    14→## 1. Project and Framework Names
    15→
    16→The Roko project has undergone several naming iterations. The following table is the
    17→canonical old-to-new mapping. All documentation uses the new names exclusively (with
    18→parenthetical notes when quoting legacy sources).
    19→
    20→| Old Name | New Name | Notes |
    21→|---|---|---|
    22→| Bardo | **Roko** | Overall framework/project name. "Bardo" was the umbrella name for the original ecosystem. |
    23→| Mori | **Roko Orchestrator** | Build/coding orchestration. Often just "orchestrator." The original Mori was a 108K-LOC TypeScript/Rust application for coding agent orchestration. |
    24→| Golem / Golems | **Agent / Agents** | The autonomous entity. "Agent" is the generic term; "Roko agent" when disambiguation is needed. The framework is Roko; individual entities are agents. |
    25→| Grimoire | **Neuro** / `roko-neuro` / **NeuroStore** | Knowledge management subsystem. Persists insights, heuristics, warnings with tier-based decay. |
    26→| Styx | **Agent Mesh** / **Mesh** | P2P relay and permissioned subnets for inter-agent communication and knowledge sharing. |
    27→| GNOS (token) | **KORAI** (mainnet) / **DAEJI** (testnet) | Token names. KORAI is the mainnet token on the Korai chain. DAEJI is the testnet equivalent on the Daeji testnet. |
    28→| Korai | **Korai** | Dedicated EVM chain for agent coordination. Mainnet. |
    29→| Daeji | **Daeji** | Testnet for the Korai chain. |
    30→| Clade | **Collective** / **Mesh** | Groups of cooperating agents. **Never use "fleet"** — this was an incorrect interim rename. |
    31→| Signal (code) | **Engram** (docs) | The universal data type. In Rust code it is `Signal` (`roko-core`). In PRD docs it is "Engram." These are **the same concept** — see note below. |
    32→
    33→> **Signal ↔ Engram:** The Rust codebase uses `Signal` as the struct name for the
    34→> universal datum (defined in `crates/roko-core/src/signal.rs`). The PRD documentation
    35→> calls the same concept an **Engram**, emphasizing its neuroscience roots (Semon 1904).
    36→> Whenever you see `Signal` in code, read it as "Engram." Whenever you see "Engram" in
    37→> docs, the corresponding Rust type is `Signal`. The six verb traits (`Substrate`,
    38→> `Scorer`, `Gate`, `Router`, `Composer`, `Policy`) all operate on `Signal` / Engram.
    39→
    40→---
    41→
    42→## 2. Configuration Files
    43→
    44→| Old | New |
    45→|---|---|
    46→| `golem.toml` | `roko.toml` |
    47→
    48→---
    49→
    50→## 3. Crate Names
    51→
    52→| Old Crate | New Crate | Notes |
    53→|---|---|---|
    54→| All `golem-*` | `roko-*` | Mechanical rename. |
    55→| All `bardo-*` | `roko-*` | Mechanical rename. Note: `bardo-runtime` and `bardo-primitives` still use old names in the current codebase. Rename is planned. |
    56→| `mori-index` | `roko-index` | Code parsing, symbol graphs, HDC fingerprints. |
    57→| `mori-context` | `roko-compose` + `roko-index` | Context features moved to `roko-compose`; code intelligence moved to `roko-index`. |
    58→| `mori-mcp` | `roko-mcp-{stdio,github,slack,scripts}` | Split into per-transport MCP server crates. |
    59→| `bardo-terminal` | `roko-cli` | The terminal UI is in `roko-cli`, with a separate TUI scaffold. |
    60→| `roko-golem` | **DISSOLVED** | See Crate Dissolution section below. |
    61→
    62→---
    63→
    64→## 4. Crate Dissolution: `roko-golem`
    65→
    66→The `roko-golem` crate has been dissolved. Its subsystems are redistributed to standalone
    67→crates following the composability principle: any subsystem can pipe to any other through
    68→Engrams and the six Synapse traits. No umbrella crate is needed.
    69→
    70→| Subsystem | Original Location | New Location | Notes |
    71→|---|---|---|---|
    72→| Daimon (972 lines, fully implemented) | `roko-golem/daimon.rs` | `roko-daimon` | Full PAD vector implementation, behavioral states, somatic markers. |
    73→| Dreams (43 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/dreams.rs` | `roko-dreams` | Placeholder deleted; `roko-dreams` is the expanded implementation. |
    74→| Grimoire (44 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/grimoire.rs` | `roko-neuro` | Placeholder deleted; `roko-neuro` is the replacement with tier-based knowledge management. |
    75→| Chain Witness (43 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/chain_witness.rs` | `roko-chain` as `chain_witness` module | Moved. |
    76→| Mortality (44 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/mortality.rs` | **DELETED ENTIRELY** | No mortality system in the new architecture. Resource constraints (budget, confidence, time) replace mortality clocks. |
    77→| Hypnagogia (42 lines, placeholder) | `roko-golem/hypnagogia.rs` | `roko-dreams` as `hypnagogia` module | Moved to the Dreams crate. |
    78→| `ScaffoldEngine` trait | `roko-golem/lib.rs` | **DELETED** | Each subsystem defines its own trait. No umbrella needed. |
    79→| `GolemScaffold` aggregator | `roko-golem/lib.rs` | **DELETED** | Composition happens at the application layer via configuration. |
    80→
    81→After dissolution, `roko-golem` is removed from workspace members in `Cargo.toml`.
    82→
    83→**Composability principle**: Any subsystem can pipe to any other. Daimon emits Engrams →
    84→Neuro stores them. Dreams reads from Neuro → produces new Engrams. Chain posts Engrams
    85→on-chain. Everything flows through the six Synapse traits.
    86→
    87→---
    88→
    89→## 5. Core Types
    90→
    91→| Old Name | New Name | Notes |
    92→|---|---|---|
    93→| `Signal` (as architectural noun) | **Engram** | The canonical architectural term. Used in all documentation. |
    94→| `Signal` (as Rust type name) | `Signal` (for now) | The Rust type is still named `Signal` in the current codebase. Rename to `Engram` is Tier 0D in the implementation plan. Documentation uses "Engram" with a note about the current code name. |
    95→| `SignalBuilder` | **EngramBuilder** | Builder pattern for Engram construction. Code currently uses `SignalBuilder`. |
    96→| `signal.rs` | `engram.rs` | Source file rename. |
    97→| "1 noun, 6 verbs" | **Synapse Architecture** | Architecture branding. The six traits are the "synapses" through which Engrams flow. |
    98→
    99→---
   100→
   101→## 6. Interface Names
   102→
   103→| Old | New |
   104→|---|---|
   105→| Bardo Sanctum | **Roko Portal** (web dashboard) |
   106→| bardo-terminal / Bardo | **Roko TUI** (terminal dashboard) |
   107→| Mori TUI | **Roko TUI** |
   108→
   109→---
   110→
   111→## 7. Token Details
   112→
   113→| Token | Chain | Notes |
   114→|---|---|---|
   115→| **KORAI** | Korai (mainnet) | 1% annual demurrage. Replaces GNOS. |
   116→| **DAEJI** | Daeji (testnet) | Testnet equivalent of KORAI. |
   117→
   118→When a legacy document mentions "GNOS token," the correct new name is "KORAI token" (or
   119→"DAEJI token" if explicitly about testnet). When it mentions "golem chain," the correct
   120→new name is "Korai chain."
   121→
   122→---
   123→
   124→## 8. Subsystem Names — Kept Unchanged
   125→
   126→These names are preserved from the original architecture with no rename needed:
   127→
   128→| Name | What It Is |
   129→|---|---|
   130→| **Mirage** / **mirage-rs** | In-process EVM simulator for transaction testing. |
   131→| **Heartbeat** | The agent's cognitive loop — one complete cycle of perceive → evaluate → attend → integrate → act → verify → persist → adapt → meta-cognize. |
   132→| **CoALA** | Cognitive Architecture for Language Agents (Sumers et al. 2023). The theoretical framework underlying the universal cognitive loop. |
   133→| **Pheromone system** | Stigmergic coordination: agents leave decaying signals in shared substrate for indirect communication (Grassé 1959, Parunak et al. 2007). |
   134→| **Sleepwalker** | Reduced-capability sleep mode for agents during low-activity periods. |
   135→| **Oneirography** | Recording and analysis of Dreams cycle outputs. |
   136→| **Hypnagogia** | The transitional state between waking and sleeping cognition, implemented as a creative hypothesis generator. |
   137→| **ALMA** | Three-layer affect model (Gebhard 2005) informing the Daimon's emotional processing. |
   138→| **Somatic markers** | Damasio's somatic marker hypothesis (Damasio 1994): emotional signals from past experience that bias decision-making. |
   139→| **Library of Babel** | Cross-collective knowledge sharing — named after Borges' infinite library concept. |
   140→| **Bazaar** | Commerce primitives for agent-to-agent economic interaction. |
   141→| **MPP** | Machine Payment Protocol — protocol for agent-initiated micropayments. |
   142→| **Testament** | Knowledge transfer between agents (repurposed from "death inheritance" to "knowledge export/import"). |
   143→| **Portal** | Interface concept for user interaction surfaces. |
   144→| **Spectre** | The procedurally generated creature visualization — a dot-cloud entity whose body encodes cognitive state. |
   145→| **ROSEDUST** | Dark-only design language (rose on void-black). The visual identity system for all Roko interfaces. |
   146→
   147→---
   148→
   149→## 9. New Names (Not in Legacy Sources)
   150→
   151→These terms are introduced in the new architecture and do not appear in legacy documents:
   152→
   153→| Term | Definition |
   154→|---|---|
   155→| **Engram** | The core data type — a content-addressed, scored, decaying, lineage-tracked unit of cognition. Replaces "Signal" as the architectural noun. |
   156→| **Synapse Architecture** | The 6-trait composition model. Replaces "1 noun, 6 verbs." |
   157→| **Five Layers** | Runtime (L0) / Framework (L1) / Scaffold (L2) / Harness (L3) / Orchestration (L4). |
   158→| **Cognitive Cross-Cuts** | Neuro / Daimon / Dreams — subsystems injected across multiple layers. |
   159→| **C-Factor** | Collective intelligence ratio metric (Woolley et al. 2010, Science 330). |
   160→| **C-Score** | Composite optimization metric for collective performance. |
   161→| **Three Cognitive Speeds** | Gamma (~5-15s reactive) / Theta (~75s reflective) / Delta (hours consolidation). |
   162→| **16 T0 Probes** | Zero-LLM diagnostic probes that suppress ~80% of ticks to T0 (no inference call). |
   163→| **VCG Attention Auction** | Vickrey-Clarke-Groves mechanism for truthful context budget allocation. |
   164→| **Somatic Landscape** | k-d tree over 8D strategy space with 15% contrarian retrieval for diversity. |
   165→| **Hypnagogia Engine** | Creative hypothesis generator: Thalamic Gate + Executive Loosener + Dali Interrupt + Homuncular Observer. |
   166→| **Cognitive Kernel Primitives** | Namespaces, cognitive signals, scheduling, syscalls — the "operating system" layer. |
   167→| **Korai Passport** | ERC-721 soulbound agent identity on the Korai chain. |
   168→| **Spore / Sparrow** | Job market protocols for agent task discovery and claiming. |
   169→| **ISFR** | Intersubjective Fact Registry — decentralized fact verification. |
   170→| **Valhalla** | Privacy layer using TEE, PSI (Private Set Intersection), and ZK proofs. |
   171→
   172→---
   173→
   174→## 10. Glossary of Architectural Terms
   175→
   176→### A
   177→
   178→| Term | Definition |
   179→|---|---|
   180→| **A2A** | Agent-to-Agent protocol. Google's open standard for inter-agent communication. |
   181→| **A2UI** | Agent-to-User-Interface. Agents generate their own UI components in ROSEDUST. |
   182→| **Active Inference** | Framework from neuroscience (Friston 2010): self-organizing systems minimize prediction error through perception and action. Roko uses this as a conceptual framework for attention and tier routing. |
   183→| **ADAS** | Automated Design of Agentic Systems (Hu et al. ICLR 2025). Meta-agent that searches the space of agent architectures. |
   184→| **Adaptive Clock** | The scheduler that manages three cognitive speeds (Gamma/Theta/Delta) and adjusts cadence based on affect state and task characteristics. |
   185→| **Agent Mesh** | P2P communication and knowledge sharing between Roko agents. Replaces "Styx." |
   186→| **Attestation** | Cryptographic proof of Engram origin — Ed25519 signature with optional chain attestation. |
   187→
   188→### B
   189→
   190→| Term | Definition |
   191→|---|---|
   192→| **Body** | The typed payload of an Engram. Variants: Empty (marker), Text (UTF-8), Json (structured), Bytes (binary). |
   193→| **Budget** | Resource constraints for Composer operations: max_tokens, max_signals, max_bytes, max_wall_ms. |
   194→
   195→### C
   196→
   197→| Term | Definition |
   198→|---|---|
   199→| **C-Factor** | Collective intelligence ratio: `C-Factor = Collective Performance / Sum(Individual Performances)`. Values > 1.0 indicate superlinear intelligence. (Woolley et al. 2010) |
   200→| **C-Score** | Composite optimization metric: `gate_pass×0.3 + cost_efficiency×0.2 + speed×0.15 + first_try_rate×0.25 + knowledge_growth×0.1`. |
   201→| **CascadeRouter** | Multi-stage model routing: confidence threshold → contextual bandit (LinUCB) → cost-aware selection. Persisted to `.roko/learn/cascade-router.json`. |
   202→| **Cognitive Loop** | The 9-step universal loop: PERCEIVE → EVALUATE → ATTEND → INTEGRATE → ACT → VERIFY → PERSIST → ADAPT → META-COGNIZE. |
   203→| **Collective** | A group of cooperating agents. Replaces "Clade." |
   204→| **Composer** | Synapse trait. Combines multiple Engrams into one new Engram under Budget constraints. Synchronous. Takes `&dyn Scorer`. |
   205→| **ContentHash** | 32-byte BLAKE3 digest identifying an Engram. Computed from kind + body + author + tags. Score and decay are excluded. |
   206→| **Context** | The shared runtime environment passed to every trait method. Carries time (`now_ms`), goal, session, and extension attributes. |
   207→
   208→### D
   209→
   210→| Term | Definition |
   211→|---|---|
   212→| **Daimon** | Motivation and focus subsystem (`roko-daimon`). Maintains PAD (Pleasure-Arousal-Dominance) vectors, six behavioral states, and somatic markers. The agent's self-model. |
   213→| **Decay** | Time-based weight reduction for Engrams. Variants: None (permanent), HalfLife (exponential), Ttl (hard cutoff), Ebbinghaus (psychological forgetting curve). |
   214→| **Delta** | Consolidation cognitive speed. Hours timescale. Dreams: replay, synthesis, pruning. Knowledge tier promotion. |
   215→| **Dreams** | Offline learning subsystem (`roko-dreams`). Three-phase cycle: NREM replay + REM imagination + integration staging. |
   216→| **Dual-Process** | Kahneman's System 1/System 2 mapped to Roko: T0 (direct tool call, no LLM) / T1 (fast model) / T2 (full model, deep reasoning). |
   217→
   218→### E
   219→
   220→| Term | Definition |
   221→|---|---|
   222→| **EFE** | Expected Free Energy. `G(π) = −Pragmatic Value − Epistemic Value`. Drives tier routing without hyperparameters. |
   223→| **Engram** | The universal data type of Roko. Content-addressed (BLAKE3), scored (7-axis), decaying, lineage-tracked. Every piece of information in the system is an Engram. Currently named `Signal` in code; rename is Tier 0D. |
   224→| **EngramBuilder** | Builder pattern for constructing Engrams. Currently named `SignalBuilder` in code. |
   225→| **Episode** | A recorded sequence of agent actions and their outcomes. Stored as Engrams with `Kind::Episode`. |
   226→| **EvoSkills** | Self-evolving skill libraries via adversarial surrogate verification (Zhao et al. 2024). |
   227→
   228→### F-G
   229→
   230→| Term | Definition |
   231→|---|---|
   232→| **Forensic AI** | Content-addressed causal replay — the ability to trace any decision back to its inputs via lineage chains. Regulatory pre-compliance moat. |
   233→| **Gamma** | Reactive cognitive speed. ~5-15 second timescale. One complete loop tick: tool calls, LLM inference, verification. |
   234→| **Gate** | Synapse trait. Verifies an Engram against ground truth, producing a Verdict. Asynchronous. The bridge to external reality. |
   235→
   236→### H-K
   237→
   238→| Term | Definition |
   239→|---|---|
   240→| **HDC** | Hyperdimensional Computing. 10,240-bit vectors using XOR bind, majority bundle, Hamming similarity. Used for semantic similarity in knowledge retrieval. (Kanerva 2009, Cognitive Computation 1(2); Plate 2003; Frady et al. 2018) |
   241→| **Hypnagogia** | The creative hypothesis generator in `roko-dreams`. Four components: Thalamic Gate (stimulus filter), Executive Loosener (constraint relaxation), Dali Interrupt (capture mechanism), Homuncular Observer (coherence filter). |
   242→| **Kind** | The semantic type of an Engram. `#[non_exhaustive]` enum with variants for agent runtime, verification, tasks, context, routing, memory, observability, chain participation, and `Custom(String)` for extensions. |
   243→| **KORAI** | Mainnet token on the Korai chain. 1% annual demurrage. Replaces "GNOS." |
   244→| **Korai** | Dedicated EVM chain for agent coordination. |
   245→
   246→### L-N
   247→
   248→| Term | Definition |
   249→|---|---|
   250→| **Lineage** | A vector of ContentHashes tracking the parent Engrams from which a new Engram was derived. Forms an audit DAG. |
   251→| **Neuro** | Knowledge management subsystem (`roko-neuro`). Six knowledge types (Insight, Heuristic, Warning, CausalLink, StrategyFragment, AntiKnowledge). Four tiers (Transient, Working, Consolidated, Persistent). HDC encoding for similarity. |
   252→| **NeuroStore** | The storage backend for the Neuro subsystem. |
   253→
   254→### O-P
   255→
   256→| Term | Definition |
   257→|---|---|
   258→| **Outcome** | Feedback about a prior Router selection. Carries success/failure, reward, cost, and latency. Used by Routers to learn via bandit algorithms. |
   259→| **Pheromone** | A stigmergic signal with time-based decay. Three types: THREAT (2h half-life), OPPORTUNITY (4h), WISDOM (24h). Agents communicate indirectly by reading and writing pheromones to shared Substrates. |
   260→| **Policy** | Synapse trait. Watches Engram streams and emits new Engrams in response (interventions, reactions). Synchronous. Batch input. |
   261→| **Provenance** | Who produced an Engram and how trustworthy they are. Fields: author (String), trust ([0,1]), tainted (bool), session (Option). |
   262→
   263→### Q-R
   264→
   265→| Term | Definition |
   266→|---|---|
   267→| **Query** | Filter specification for Substrate lookups. Fields: kinds, author, session, since_ms, until_ms, min_weight, tags, limit. |
   268→| **ROSEDUST** | Dark-only design system. Rose on void-black. The visual identity for all Roko interfaces. |
   269→| **Router** | Synapse trait. Selects one Engram from candidates. Has `feedback()` method for learning from outcomes. Synchronous. |
   270→
   271→### S
   272→
   273→| Term | Definition |
   274→|---|---|
   275→| **Score** | Multi-dimensional quality assessment of an Engram. 4 stable axes: confidence [0,1], novelty [0,1], utility [0,∞), reputation [0,∞). 3 extended axes (planned): precision [0,1], salience [0,1], coherence [0,1]. Effective score formula: `confidence × (1 + novelty) × (1 + utility) × reputation`. |
   276→| **Scorer** | Synapse trait. Rates an Engram along multiple axes, producing a Score. Synchronous. Pure function of (Engram, Context). |
   277→| **Selection** | Output of a Router: the chosen Engram's ContentHash, confidence, router name, and optional reasoning. |
   278→| **Spectre** | Procedurally generated dot-cloud creature per agent. Body encodes cognitive state; eyes encode emotion; clarity encodes prediction accuracy. |
   279→| **Substrate** | Synapse trait. Stores and queries Engrams. Asynchronous. Multiple backends: MemorySubstrate (testing), FileSubstrate (JSONL persistence), HdcSubstrate (semantic search), ChainSubstrate (on-chain state). |
   280→| **Synapse Architecture** | The compositional model underlying Roko: one noun (Engram) + six verb traits (Substrate, Scorer, Gate, Router, Composer, Policy). |
   281→
   282→### T-V
   283→
   284→| Term | Definition |
   285→|---|---|
   286→| **T0 / T1 / T2** | Inference tiers. T0: no LLM call (direct tool call, ~80% of ticks). T1: fast model, shallow reasoning (~15%). T2: full model, deep reasoning (~5%). Routing emerges from active inference (EFE). |
   287→| **Theta** | Reflective cognitive speed. ~75 second timescale. Summarize recent work, update Daimon state, check predictions. |
   288→| **TickOutcome** | The result of one `loop_tick` invocation. Contains: candidates_examined, composed Engram, Verdict, emitted Engrams, written ContentHashes. |
   289→| **Verdict** | Output of a Gate. Contains: passed (bool), reason (String), gate name, score [0,1], optional detail, optional TestCount, optional error_digest, duration_ms. |
   290→
   291→---
   292→
   293→## 11. Naming Application Rules
   294→
   295→1. **When quoting legacy sources verbatim**: Keep the old name in the quote but add a
   296→   parenthetical: "(formerly Grimoire, now Neuro)."
   297→2. **When paraphrasing**: Use the new name directly.
   298→3. **Code samples**: Use new crate names (`roko-primitives`, `roko-runtime`, etc.) even if
   299→   the current Rust code still uses old names. Note the current name in a comment.
   300→4. **Struct/type names**: Say "Engram" in prose. In Rust code snippets, use `Signal` and
   301→   add a comment like `// will be renamed to Engram in Tier 0D`.
   302→5. **File paths**: All `bardo-*` → `roko-*`, all `mori-*` → `roko-*`.
   303→6. **Never say**: "Golem SDK" → say "Agent SDK" or "Roko SDK".
   304→7. **Never say**: "Mori + Golem" → say "Roko framework with coding and chain domain plugins."
   305→
   306→---
   307→
   308→## Academic Foundations
   309→
   310→| Citation | Relevance |
   311→|---|---|
   312→| Woolley et al. 2010, Science 330(6004) | C-Factor: collective intelligence metric. |
   313→| Sumers et al. 2023 (arXiv:2309.02427) | CoALA: cognitive architecture framework. |
   314→| Friston 2010, Nature Reviews Neuroscience 11(2) | Free Energy Principle, active inference. |
   315→| Kanerva 2009, Cognitive Computation 1(2) | Hyperdimensional Computing foundations. |
   316→| Grassé 1959 | Stigmergy: indirect coordination via environmental signals. |
   317→| Damasio 1994, Descartes' Error | Somatic markers: emotion biasing decision-making. |
   318→| Mehrabian & Russell 1974 | PAD model: Pleasure-Arousal-Dominance emotional space. |
   319→| Gebhard 2005 | ALMA: three-layer affect model. |
   320→| Kahneman 2011, Thinking, Fast and Slow | Dual-process theory: System 1 / System 2. |
   321→
   322→---
   323→
   324→## Cross-References
   325→
   326→- [00-vision-and-thesis.md](00-vision-and-thesis.md) — Why the architecture exists
   327→- [02-engram-data-type.md](02-engram-data-type.md) — Full Engram specification
   328→- [03-score-7-axis-appraisal.md](03-score-7-axis-appraisal.md) — Score details
   329→- [06-synapse-traits.md](06-synapse-traits.md) — The six Synapse traits
   330→

input.prompt:
Scan ALL 398 .md files in /Users/will/dev/nunchi/roko/roko/docs/ for broken internal links.

Internal links are markdown links like [text](path.md) or [text](../section/file.md) that reference other docs within the docs/ directory.

Steps:
1. Use Grep to find all markdown links: pattern `\[.*?\]\((?!http).*?\.md.*?\)` or similar
2. For each linked file, verify the target exists using Glob
3. Report any broken links with:
   - Source file path and line number
   - The broken link text
   - What the link points to
   - Whether the target file exists or not

Also check for:
- Links to `../` paths that escape the docs directory incorrectly  
- Links using old section numbers that may have been renumbered
- Links to INDEX.md files that don't exist in their section

Focus on catching files that are referenced but don't exist. This is more important than checking every single link.

docs/11-safety/03-taint-tracking.md:18:An autonomous agent ingests data from many sources: tool results, LLM outputs, knowledge store entries, operator inputs, external APIs, and peer agent messages. Not all of these sources are equally trustworthy. A prompt injection attack can cause the LLM to include sensitive data in its output. If that output is logged, synced to the Agent Mesh (formerly "Styx"), or broadcast, the sensitive data is exposed.

Taint tracking generalizes this principle: every piece of data carries labels describing its sensitivity and trust level, and these labels are checked before data crosses any trust boundary.

input.prompt:
Scan ALL 398 .md files in /Users/will/dev/nunchi/roko/roko/docs/ for missing '> **Implementation**: ...' annotations.

The convention is that every doc should have an Implementation status annotation near the top, typically as a blockquote like:
> **Implementation**: Shipping
> **Implementation**: Scaffold  
> **Implementation**: Specified (not started)
> **Implementation**: Partial

Steps:
1. First, get a list of ALL .md files in docs/
2. Use Grep to find files that DO have the annotation: pattern `> \*\*Implementation\*\*`
3. Compare against the full list to find files MISSING the annotation
4. Report the list of files missing the annotation

Exclude INDEX.md files (they are just indexes) and the root-level docs (INDEX.md, STATUS.md, QUICKSTART.md, COMPARISON.md) from the "missing" list since those are meta-docs.

Report the complete list of files missing the Implementation annotation.

docs/10-dreams/17-advanced-dream-concepts.md:31:A fleet of agents operating on related tasks accumulates complementary dream insights. Without sharing, each agent independently re-derives threat patterns and heuristics that other agents have already rehearsed. Dream sharing propagates insights across the fleet, compressing the time to collective competence.
/Users/will/dev/nunchi/roko/roko/docs/10-dreams/17-advanced-dream-concepts.md-32-
docs/10-dreams/17-advanced-dream-concepts.md-33-The challenge is that dream content is inherently agent-specific: it is grounded in each agent's episode history, role context, and confidence calibration. Naive broadcasting of raw hypotheses produces noise. The dream sharing protocol must be selective, confidence-aware, and privacy-preserving.

docs/13-coordination/INDEX.md:51:| 01 | [01-stigmergy-beyond-termites.md](01-stigmergy-beyond-termites.md) | Stigmergy Beyond Termites | ~280 | Biological examples (ants, bees, bacteria, spiders), human stigmergy (Wikipedia, science, cities, OSS), computational stigmergy (ACO, PSO, swarm robotics), software engineering (code smells, niche construction, information foraging), Constructal Law, self-organized criticality |
docs/13-coordination/INDEX.md:52:| 02 | [02-git-as-stigmergy.md](02-git-as-stigmergy.md) | Git as Stigmergy | ~250 | Repository as stigmergic environment, sematectonic (code structure) and marker-based (commits, branches, CI) signals, multi-agent worktree model, base+overlay pattern, declared contracts, pheromone traces in codebases |
docs/13-coordination/INDEX.md:53:| 03 | [03-digital-pheromones.md](03-digital-pheromones.md) | Digital Pheromones | ~490 | Pheromone struct, exponential decay formula, decay profiles per kind, confirmation mechanics, anti-spoofing via reputation, pheromone field operations, pheromone-enriched context assembly, complete lifecycle, **pheromone interference model** (SINR framework, cross-kind interference matrix, anti-saturation mechanisms, mitigation strategies) |
docs/13-coordination/INDEX.md:54:| 04 | [04-pheromone-kinds.md](04-pheromone-kinds.md) | Pheromone Kinds | ~450 | PheromoneKind enum (Threat/Opportunity/Wisdom/Alpha/Pattern/Anomaly/Consensus/Custom), three-tier taxonomy, kind interactions, Alpha paradox, Pattern→Wisdom promotion, Consensus stability, **pheromone-driven task allocation** (response threshold model, Hill function, emergent division of labor) |
docs/13-coordination/INDEX.md:55:| 05 | [05-pheromone-scope.md](05-pheromone-scope.md) | Pheromone Scope | ~260 | PheromoneScope enum (Local/Mesh/Global), three-level hierarchy, Constructal Law connection, trust discounting, scope promotion, cross-scope composition, permissioned subnets preview |
docs/13-coordination/INDEX.md:56:| 06 | [06-agent-mesh-sync.md](06-agent-mesh-sync.md) | Agent Mesh Sync | ~470 | Dual-transport architecture (WebSocket + Iroh), EventFabric integration, connection registry, store-and-forward, iroh-gossip pheromone propagation, iroh-blobs knowledge exchange, ERC-8004 discovery, failure modes, security model, **partition tolerance** (AP design, partition-aware morphogenetics, post-partition reconciliation, Byzantine agent detection) |
docs/13-coordination/INDEX.md:57:| 07 | [07-morphogenetic-specialization.md](07-morphogenetic-specialization.md) | Morphogenetic Specialization | ~660 | Turing 1952 reaction-diffusion, strategy concentration vector (8 dimensions), Gierer-Meinhardt kinetics, update rule with activation/inhibition/decay/noise, niche competition, role coordination messages, resource pressure modulation, convergence analysis, **Turing pattern stability analysis** (linear stability, pitchfork bifurcation, Hopf oscillatory instability, Lyapunov stability monitoring, pattern selection), DeLanda assemblage theory |
docs/13-coordination/INDEX.md:58:| 08 | [08-permissioned-subnets.md](08-permissioned-subnets.md) | Permissioned Subnets | ~240 | Private Mesh scopes, access control models (invite/role/reputation), internal reputation, opt-in publishing, scope boundary enforcement, organizational patterns, club goods theory |

docs/16-heartbeat/08-dual-process-t0-t1-t2.md:140:`ProbeResult::is_anomalous()` returns `true` when a probe's measured value exceeds a per-probe z-score threshold. Each of the 16 T0 probes (see [09-16-t0-probes.md](./09-16-t0-probes.md)) maintains a rolling mean and standard deviation over its last 100 measurements. A measurement is anomalous if `|value - mean| > 2.0 * stddev`. The count is simply the number of probes returning `true` on this tick.
docs/16-heartbeat/08-dual-process-t0-t1-t2.md:344:Playbook rules are learned heuristics stored in the `PlaybookStore` (see topic [05-learning](../05-learning/INDEX.md)). Each rule has a condition, a confidence score, and an action. At T0, the agent checks all rules against the current tick state.
docs/16-heartbeat/08-dual-process-t0-t1-t2.md:454:**Top-5 Neuro entry selection.** The `PredictiveScorer` (see [10-active-inference-compute-allocation.md](./10-active-inference-compute-allocation.md)) scores all candidate Engrams and returns the top 5. The scorer computes `salience = pragmatic_weight * utility + epistemic_value - cost_penalty` for each entry and sorts descending. Ties are broken by recency (more recent wins).
docs/16-heartbeat/08-dual-process-t0-t1-t2.md:607:See [10-active-inference-compute-allocation.md](./10-active-inference-compute-allocation.md) for the full active inference specification.

docs/16-heartbeat/00-coala-9-step-pipeline.md:117:Read the current environment state through deterministic probes. For a coding agent, this means checking build status, test results, complexity metrics, and coverage deltas. For a chain agent, this means reading prices, liquidity, position health, and gas costs. For any agent, the 16 T0 probes (see [09-16-t0-probes.md](./09-16-t0-probes.md)) run at every tick.
docs/16-heartbeat/00-coala-9-step-pipeline.md:182:This step does not exist in the universal loop — it is a domain-specific extension point between ATTEND and ACT. See [02-chain-heartbeat-variant.md](./02-chain-heartbeat-variant.md) for the full chain mapping.
